title: BlackRock Earnings: Market Gains and Record Inflows Drive AUM and Revenue to New Highs
summary: BlackRock reported record assets under management (AUM) of $14.042 trillion at the end of December 2025, driven by strong market gains and record inflows, especially into its iShares ETF platform. Morningstar analysts expect to increase their fair value estimate for BlackRock stock from $1,170 to $1,200 per share, viewing the shares as slightly undervalued despite anticipating potential headwinds from governmental policies. The firm's organic AUM growth rate of 8.6% for the fourth quarter significantly outperformed expectations and its peers.

title: Is Verizon down? Outage resolved after more than 1.5 million reports
summary: A widespread Verizon outage, impacting over 1.5 million wireless customers, was resolved late Wednesday after more than seven hours of service interruption. The company apologized for the disruption, which affected major cities nationwide, and stated that there was no indication of a cyberattack. Verizon plans to offer account credits to affected customers and advised restarting devices if issues persist.

title: OpenAI made 'conscious decision' to sit out Apple Siri deal: report (OPENAI:Private)
summary: OpenAI reportedly made a "conscious decision" not to partner with Apple for its updated Siri, despite speculation that Apple passed on the ChatGPT maker. Instead, Apple chose Google's Gemini models for a multi-year partnership involving significant payments. OpenAI's decision was influenced by its focus on developing its own AI hardware and surpassing other large tech companies, though it continues to partner with Apple through ChatGPT integrations and is recruiting former Apple talent.

title: WestEnd Advisors LLC Has $53.84 Million Stock Holdings in Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL
summary: WestEnd Advisors LLC reduced its stake in Alphabet Inc. by 10.7% in Q3, now holding 221,451 shares valued at $53.84 million, representing 1.3% of its portfolio. Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) reported strong Q3 earnings, exceeding revenue and EPS estimates, trading near its 52-week high with a $4.05 trillion market cap and a "Moderate Buy" consensus analyst rating. The company's stock is driven by AI advancements but faces operational and regulatory challenges, alongside significant insider selling activity.

title: Goldman Sachs Posts Strong 2025 Results, Raises Dividend
summary: Goldman Sachs reported strong financial results for 2025, with net revenues up 9% and diluted EPS reaching $51.32, leading to a dividend increase to $4.50 per share. The growth was primarily driven by its Global Banking & Markets division, which offset challenges in Platform Solutions related to the Apple Card portfolio. The company maintained a 15.0% return on equity and saw its book value per share grow by 6.2%.
